name: Woods v. Donald
question: Did the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit properly affirm the granting of federal habeas relief by holding that the Michigan Court of Appeals improperly applied Supreme Court precedent?
description: A case in which the Court held that  federal habeas relief may only be granted when a state court’s decision runs counter to clearly established federal law, and there is no precedent clearly establishing that the absence of counsel during testimony about codefendants violates a defendant’s Sixth Amendment right to effective assistance of counsel.
